---
title: "Workshop Datasets"
---

This page describes the datasets the workshop uses, where to download them, and where to put them so the tutorials can find them.

## Expected directory layout

By convention every tutorial reads from `../data/` --- i.e. a `data/` folder at the repository root. After you finish downloading, your tree should look like:

```
scrnaseq_workshop/
├── tutorials/
│   ├── Tutorial_01_QC_Preprocessing.qmd
│   └── ...
└── data/
    ├── filtered_feature_bc_matrix/                  # primary scRNA-seq
    │   ├── barcodes.tsv.gz
    │   ├── features.tsv.gz
    │   └── matrix.mtx.gz
    └── atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/                       # bonus scATAC-seq (optional)
        ├── filtered_peak_bc_matrix/
        │   ├── barcodes.tsv
        │   ├── matrix.mtx
        │   └── peaks.bed
        └── fragments.tsv.gz (+ .tbi)
```

If your layout differs, adjust the `data.dir` argument in the `Read10X*()` calls inside each tutorial.

## Primary dataset --- 20k NSCLC DTC (10x 3' v3.1 Multiplex)

**What it is.** Twenty-thousand dissociated tumor cells (DTCs) pooled from **seven non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) donors**, run on the 10x Chromium **3' v3.1** assay with **CellPlex multiplexing** (CMO hashing). Every cell carries a gene-expression profile *and* a sample-of-origin barcode --- so downstream you get both heterogeneity and donor-level structure to integrate.

-   Landing page: [10x Genomics --- 20k NSCLC DTC, 3' v3.1 Multiplex](https://www.10xgenomics.com/resources/datasets/20-k-mixture-of-nsclc-dt-cs-from-7-donors-3-v-3-1-multiplex-3-1-standard-6-0-0)
-   Used in: Tutorials 01--04, plus most lecture examples.

### Download

``` bash
# from your project root
mkdir -p data && cd data

curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-exp/6.0.0/20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex/20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex_count_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.tar.gz

tar -xzf 20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex_count_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.tar.gz

# Sanity check — should print three .gz files
ls filtered_feature_bc_matrix/
```

## Bonus dataset --- PBMC 10k scATAC-seq v1.1

Used only for the **scATAC-seq with Signac** bonus exercise.

-   Landing page: [10x Genomics --- PBMC 10k scATAC-seq v1.1](https://www.10xgenomics.com/resources/datasets/10-k-peripheral-blood-mononuclear-cells-pbm-cs-from-a-healthy-donor-next-gem-v-1-1-1-1-standard-2-0-0)

``` bash
# from your project root, into data/
cd data

curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_filtered_peak_bc_matrix.tar.gz
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_fragments.tsv.gz
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_fragments.tsv.gz.tbi
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_singlecell.csv

tar -xzf atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_filtered_peak_bc_matrix.tar.gz
```

## Reference / annotation datasets

These are loaded automatically by the relevant R packages on first use --- you don't need to download them manually, but they do need an internet connection the first time.

| Reference | Loaded by | Used in |
|---|---|---|
| `HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData()` | `celldex` | Tutorial 05 (SingleR annotation) |
| Azimuth `pbmcref` (or NSCLC ref) | `Azimuth` / `SeuratData` | Tutorial 05 |
| `EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86` | Bioconductor | scATAC-seq bonus |

## Disk-space budget

| Dataset | Compressed | Extracted |
|---|---|---|
| 20k NSCLC DTC (filtered counts) | \~250 MB | \~700 MB |
| PBMC 10k scATAC-seq | \~2.5 GB | \~5 GB |
| Reference data (cached) | --- | \~500 MB |

Plan for **\~6 GB total** if you're doing both scRNA and scATAC.

## "I can't download from 10x"

10x Genomics public datasets are free but require accepting a terms-of-use click-through. If `curl` fails, open the landing-page link in a browser, click *Download*, accept the terms, and you'll get a direct URL you can `wget`/`curl` from then on.

If you're behind a restrictive network, all of the files above are also mirrored on the workshop's Talapas project directory `/projects/<PIRG>/scrnaseq_workshop/data/` once your account is set up --- copy from there with `scp` or work directly on the cluster.
